player_notes.donovan mitchell: Scored 43 points (39 in second half) in Game 4 win vs. Pistons — series now tied 2-2 heading to Game 5 in Detroit. Extremely hot hand; high confidence on points prop.

game_notes.CLE vs DET: Series tied 2-2 heading into Game 5 in Detroit. Mitchell is scorching hot, but expect elevated defensive intensity from Pistons at home.

suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update LAL playoff status: eliminated in R2 (swept by OKC 4-0). LeBron's 2025-26 playoff run is over. Add note that his future with LAL is uncertain — he is 'recalibrating' and has not committed to returning. Remove any R2+ projection language for LAL players.

Wemby had 27 points, 17 rebounds, 3 blocks. SAS leads series 3-2. Anthony Edwards and MIN are facing elimination ('Defiant Edwards says Wolves not worried with season on brink'). suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add SAS vs MIN R2 series diary. Current series state: SAS leads 3-2 after G5 (126-97 SAS win). Wemby: 27/17/3 blocks in G5. suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: Injury landscape entry: 'Victor Wembanyama (SAS) — SERIES OVER (SAS advances). Concussion protocol cleared before G4; G4: 27/12/3 (9-17 FG, 1 3PM, 34 min) and G5: 17/14/3 (5-7 FG, 1 3PM, 34 min) confirm full health and no residual restriction from the jaw injury. Right ankle pain did not limit performance in either return game. Treat as fully healthy entering R2. No injury concern.'
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms Wemby suffered his first career ejection in a game prior to G5 of the MIN series (an elbow to Naz Reid), then 'returned with a huge performance' in G5: 27 points, 17 rebounds, 3 blocks. The NBA did not suspend him. The injury landscape entry above describes R1 vs POR (his G4/G5 were in the POR series), but R2 G5 stats are now available and the ejection incident is new information not in the context doc.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Update Wembanyama's active injury/incident log for R2: He received his first career ejection (elbow to Naz Reid) in the MIN series — NBA reviewed and took no suspension action. G5 vs MIN: 27/17/3 blocks, fully healthy, no minutes restriction. The ejection concern is resolved. Treat as fully healthy for G6.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The season context document has no R2 series diary for CLE vs DET. The playoff bracket section shows both teams advanced from R1 but contains no R2 series state.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: News confirms CLE vs DET is an active R2 series. 'The Cavaliers and Pistons are headed back to Detroit tied 2-2' (pre-G4 preview), and then Donovan Mitchell 'tied an NBA playoff record with 39 points in the second half' as 'Cavs evened their second-round series against the Pistons with a 112-103 victory Monday night' — meaning the series is now tied 2-2 after G4. G5 preview article also confirms '2026 NBA playoffs: What to watch for in Cavaliers-Pistons Game 5' with the series tied 2-2 heading back to Detroit.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add CLE vs DET R2 series diary. Current series state: tied 2-2 after G4. Mitchell scored 43 points total in G4 (39 in the second half, tying an NBA playoff record for points in a second half). Pistons coach Bickerstaff called the FT disparity in G4 'unacceptable.' Series shifts back to Detroit for G5. Update playoff bracket accordingly.

suggested_context_updates.current_context_fact: The R1 series diary notes Mitchell's persistent shooting slump across G3-G7 in the TOR series: 'Mitchell's persistent shooting slump across G3-G7 (five consecutive sub-30 games after back-to-back 30+ performances in G1-G2) is CLE's most critical tactical variable.' R2 context for Mitchell is absent.
suggested_context_updates.conflict: Mitchell scored 43 points in R2 G4 vs DET (39 in the second half), tying an NBA playoff record for second-half scoring. This directly contradicts any residual 'shooting slump' narrative and establishes a new R2 performance ceiling. His R1 slump has clearly not carried into R2.
suggested_context_updates.suggested_action: Add Mitchell R2 context: His R1 shooting slump did not carry into R2. G4 vs DET: 43 points (39 in the second half), tying an NBA playoff record. Treat Mitchell as operating at or above his G1-G2 R1 ceiling. Remove 'shooting slump' framing as an active concern for R2 picks.
